our_story: Ai's home prefecture — where the challenge began. Our first trip in October 2024 set the tone: torisashi and oden at Wakana, Sakurajima brooding across the bay from Senganen garden, the quiet grandeur of Tsurumaru Castle. Later visits went deeper — buried in volcanic sand at Ibusuki, standing in silence at the Shimazu clan graves, and spending evenings lost in rare Kagoshima shochu at Wasou. Home is never boring.
our_story_ja: チャレンジのスタート地点にして、藍の故郷。2024年10月の初訪問では、若菜で鳥刺しとおでんを楽しみ、仙巌園から桜島を眺め、鶴丸城の風格に圧倒されました。その後の訪問では、指宿の砂むし、福昌寺跡の島津家墓所、和総での希少な焼酎と、さらに深く鹿児島を知ることができました。ホームは決して飽きない。

entries.name: Kagoshima Plaza Hotel Tenmonkan
entries.name_ja: かごしまプラザホテル天文館
entries.our_notes: Our first ever hotel stay in Kagoshima — right in the heart of Tenmonkan. Perfectly located for exploring the tram network, the covered shopping arcade, and the izakayas around Tenmonkan. Nothing fancy, but comfortable and clean with that slightly retro Japanese business hotel feel.
entries.our_notes_ja: 鹿児島初の宿泊は天文館のど真ん中。路面電車や天文館のアーケード、居酒屋街へのアクセスが抜群。ちょっとレトロなビジネスホテルの雰囲気ですが、清潔で快適でした。
entries.background: Tenmonkan (天文館) takes its name from the Meidōkan astronomical observatory built by 25th Shimazu lord Shigehide in 1779 — one of the earliest Western-style observatories in Japan. Today it is Kagoshima's main entertainment district, centred on a long covered arcade that stretches from the tram stop. The name is a constant reminder that Satsuma was unusually open to Western learning even in the Edo period.

photos.caption: かごしまプラザホテル天文館 — our first Kagoshima base

entries.name: Wakana Izakaya
entries.our_notes: A proper Kagoshima izakaya near Tenmonkan. We had torisashi — raw chicken sashimi, seared lightly on the outside, served with ginger and lemon — a signature Kagoshima dish you won't find easily outside the prefecture. Also had excellent oden with daikon and chicken, beautifully presented on a blue-and-white Satsuma-style bowl. Kagoshima fish sashimi platter too, beautifully plated on a painted dish with wasabi.
entries.our_notes_ja: 天文館近くの本格鹿児島居酒屋。鹿児島名物の「鳥刺し」は、表面だけ軽くあぶった生の鶏肉を生姜とレモンで。県外ではなかなか食べられない名物です。大根と鶏肉のおでんも白地に青絵の薩摩焼風の器で登場。鮮魚の刺盛りも絵付けの皿で美しく盛り付けられていました。
entries.background: Torisashi (鳥刺し) — raw chicken sashimi — is Kagoshima's most distinctive local food and largely unavailable elsewhere due to strict food safety laws. Kagoshima has special exemptions rooted in generations of experience with Satsuma Jidori (薩摩地鶏) — a robust local breed with naturally lower bacterial risk. The chickens are slaughtered and processed under strict hygiene protocols and served within hours. It's eaten with ginger, soy sauce and sesame oil. First-timers are often shocked; converts are evangelical.

photos.caption: 鳥刺し (Torisashi) — raw Satsuma chicken sashimi with ginger and lemon
Fresh fish sashimi platter on a painted Satsuma-style dish
おでん — oden with daikon and chicken on a classic blue-and-white bowl

entries.name: Tsurumaru Castle (Kagoshima Castle)
entries.our_notes: The restored Nishinotaisho Gate — all heavy timber and white plaster — makes a striking approach across the moat bridge. The castle itself was deliberately modest: the Shimazu clan said that the true castle was the mountains and the sea, not stone walls. A philosophy as confident as it is elegant. The moat had lotus growing in it.
entries.our_notes_ja: 復元された西之丸大手御門は重厚な木材と白漆喰が美しく、石橋を渡る先にそびえる姿は圧巻。しかし島津家はあえて天守閣を持たなかった。「山を城とし、海を堀とす」という思想がいかにも薩摩らしい。お堀には蓮の葉が広がっていました。
entries.background: Kagoshima Castle (鹿児島城), also called Tsurumaru-jō (鶴丸城) for its flat, crane-like silhouette, was constructed in 1601 by Shimazu Iehisa and deliberately built without a keep (天守閣). The Shimazu believed natural geography — the mountains behind and sea in front — was sufficient defence. It was partially burned during the 1877 Satsuma Rebellion led by Saigo Takamori. The West Outer Gate (西之丸大手御門) was painstakingly reconstructed in 2020 using traditional techniques.

photos.caption: 西之丸大手御門 — the reconstructed main gate of Tsurumaru Castle
Alan at the castle gate — lotus moat and Sakurajima beyond

entries.name: Shiroyama — Terukuni Shrine & Viewpoint
entries.name_ja: 城山 — 照国神社と展望台
entries.our_notes: Climbed Shiroyama hill to the famous observation deck — and despite grey clouds the view of the city with Sakurajima across the bay was extraordinary. You can feel why this place matters so much to Kagoshima people. On the way, visited Terukuni Shrine, the green-roofed sanctuary dedicated to Lord Nariakira, with its distinctive pink columns and Shimazu family crest lanterns. Rainy but perfect.
entries.our_notes_ja: 城山の展望台まで登ると、雨曇りにもかかわらず桜島と鹿児島市内の絶景が広がっていました。この景色が鹿児島の人たちにとって特別なものだとよくわかります。途中で照国神社にも参拝。島津斉彬公を祀る緑の屋根と薄ピンクの柱が印象的な神社で、島津家の家紋入り提灯が並んでいました。雨でも最高！
entries.background: Shiroyama (城山 — 'Castle Mountain') was the final refuge of Saigo Takamori and his rebel samurai in the Satsuma Rebellion of 1877. Outnumbered and surrounded by Imperial troops, Saigo was mortally wounded (or died by seppuku — accounts differ) on 24 September 1877, ending the last major samurai uprising. Terukuni Shrine (照国神社) was established in 1863 to enshrine the spirit of Shimazu Nariakira (1809–1858), the visionary 28th lord of Satsuma who championed modernisation and influenced the Meiji Restoration.

photos.caption: 照国神社 (Terukuni Jinja) — shrine of Lord Nariakira, with Shimazu crest lanterns
Sakurajima from Shiroyama — the classic Kagoshima view
Alan at the Shiroyama viewpoint — Sakurajima behind

entries.name: Ichiniisan — Kagoshima Shabu-Shabu
entries.name_ja: 一二三 — 鹿児島しゃぶしゃぶ
entries.our_notes: A wonderful shabu-shabu set lunch — thin Kagoshima pork slices cooked at the table in a rich miso broth, served with a wooden box of accompaniments: vegetables, egg, tofu, seasoning packets. Also came with a small cup of somen noodles topped with colourful garnishes and a big bowl of pork miso soup. Ai thoroughly approved. Generous, warming, and exactly what you want on a rainy October day.
entries.our_notes_ja: 鹿児島の薄切り豚肉を味噌ベースのスープでしゃぶしゃぶにするランチセット。木箱に野菜・卵・豆腐などが詰め込まれ、小さな素麺カップには色とりどりのトッピング、そして豚汁も付いてきます。雨の10月にぴったりの温かくてボリューム満点のランチでした。藍も大満足！
entries.background: Kagoshima is Japan's leading producer of pork (豚肉), and shabu-shabu using local Kagoshima black pork (鹿児島黒豚 / Kagoshima Kurobuta) is one of the prefecture's proudest culinary traditions. The breed was introduced to Satsuma via the Ryukyu Kingdom in the 16th century and selectively bred ever since. Kagoshima Kurobuta is prized for its rich marbling and sweetness, making it fundamentally different from standard pork — and locals will tell you this emphatically.

photos.caption: 一二三 shabu-shabu set — wooden box, miso broth, somen and pork miso soup
藍, thoroughly approving of lunch

entries.name: Senganen Garden (Iso Teien)
entries.our_notes: A stunning Shimazu clan garden right on the coast north of the city, with Sakurajima as its 'borrowed scenery' backdrop. Mossy stone pathways wind between enormous volcanic rocks and crystal-clear streams. The old villa corridor — tatami floors, shoji screens, paper lanterns — feels genuinely old and genuinely beautiful. The whole garden uses the bay as part of its composition. Unforgettable.
entries.our_notes_ja: 桜島を借景にした島津家ゆかりの庭園。苔むした石畳と玄武岩、清流が織りなす景観は美しく、古い御殿の廊下は畳と障子と紙の行灯が並ぶ本物の雰囲気。庭全体が錦江湾を取り込んだ設計で、ため息が出るほどの美しさ。
entries.background: Senganen (仙巌園) was built in 1658 by the 19th lord Shimazu Mitsuhisa as a seaside retreat. The garden pioneered the concept of 'shakkei' (借景 — borrowed scenery), incorporating Sakurajima and Kinko Bay as living elements of the composition. The adjacent Shoko Shuseikan museum was Japan's first Western-style factory, built by Shimazu Nariakira in 1851 as part of his industrial modernisation programme — a direct ancestor of the Meiji-era industrialisation that transformed Japan.

photos.caption: Stream and volcanic rock garden — Senganen
Mossy rocks and still water — Senganen
Garden and sea view — Senganen with Kinko Bay beyond
Stone bridge and sea — Senganen with Sakurajima behind
Alan and 藍 at Senganen garden
Alan and 藍 — Senganen with Kinko Bay
Alan and 藍 — Senganen
御殿の廊下 — tatami corridor of the Senganen villa
御殿の廊下 — paper lanterns and shoji screens

entries.name: Ibusuki Saraku — Natural Sand Baths
entries.name_ja: 指宿砂むし温泉 砂楽
entries.our_notes: An unforgettable experience — buried up to our necks in natural volcanic sand on the beach. The sand sits at around 50–55°C heated by geothermal springs underneath. You wear a blue yukata, staff shovel warm sand over you, and you stay in for about 10 minutes. The sign above reads 'Ojattamonse' — Kagoshima dialect for welcome!
entries.our_notes_ja: 首まで砂に埋まる、忘れられない体験！指宿の海岸に自然の地熱で温められた砂が約50〜55℃。青い浴衣を着てスタッフさんに砂をかけてもらい10分ほど入ります。「おじゃったもんせ」は鹿児島弁で「いらっしゃいませ」の意味。砂から出た後は体の芯からポカポカでした！
entries.background: Ibusuki's natural sand baths (砂むし温泉) have a history of over 300 years, making them the world's only naturally occurring beach sand baths heated entirely by geothermal springs. Kagoshima University research suggests that 10 minutes in the sand delivers the equivalent cardiovascular benefit of 3–4 times that of a regular hot spring bath. The name 'Saraku' (砂楽) comes from the Kagoshima dialect word 'sozoro aruku' meaning a leisurely, carefree stroll.

photos.caption: Ready for the sand — Ojattamonse! おじゃったもんせ！

entries.name: Fukushoji — Shimazu Clan Burial Grounds
entries.name_ja: 福昌寺跡 島津家墓地
entries.our_notes: The burial grounds of the Shimazu clan, lords of Satsuma for over 700 years. Rows of identical mossy stone lanterns flank the central grave monument behind a weathered stone torii. Atmospheric, quiet, and off the tourist trail. Fukushoji Temple itself was once one of the three largest in Japan with 1,500 monks, destroyed in the 1869 Meiji anti-Buddhist purges.
entries.our_notes_ja: 700年以上にわたって薩摩を治めた島津家歴代当主（第6代〜第28代）の墓所。石造りの鳥居の奥に苔むした石灯籠がずらりと並ぶ、静寂で圧倒的な場所。かつてここには1500人の修行僧がいた日本三大僧録所・福昌寺がありましたが、明治2年（1869年）の廃仏毀釈で廃寺となりました。
entries.background: Fukushoji was established in 1394 by the 6th Shimazu lord, Ujihisa, and grew to become one of Japan's three great Zen administrative centres with over 1,500 resident monks. The Shimazu clan ruled Satsuma domain for 29 generations spanning nearly 700 years — one of the longest continuous feudal dynasties in Japanese history. The temple was destroyed in 1869 during the Meiji government's shinbutsu bunri (神仏分離) edict, driven by aggressive anti-Buddhist sentiment in Satsuma. Only the graves and stone lanterns survive.

photos.caption: Stone torii and mossy lanterns — Shimazu clan graves, Fukushoji
寺中常に千五百余人の修行僧あり — 1,500 monks once trained here

entries.name: Wasou — Kagoshima Izakaya
entries.name_ja: 和総 — 鹿児島居酒屋
entries.our_notes: A magnificent izakaya near Tenmonkan tram stop with 100+ shochu varieties and Kagoshima home cooking. The handwritten monthly specials board listed about 20 rare limited-edition bottles with illustrated labels. We tried 華吟 (Hanagin) by Toyoshizagura — a ginjo-yeast shochu, fragrant and elegant — and 大海綾紫 (Taikai Ayamurasaki) by Taikai Shuzo — shirokoji-fermented, crisp and citrusy.
entries.our_notes_ja: 薩摩の郷土料理と希少な焼酎100種以上が揃う天文館そばの居酒屋。今月のおすすめは手書きイラスト入りで約20種の限定焼酎が紹介されていました。豊姿蔵の「華吟」と大海酒造の「大海綾紫」を楽しみました。華吟は吟醸酵母で仕込んだ優雅な香り、大海綾紫は白麹で仕込んだキレのある柑橘系の味わい。
entries.background: Wasou is run by a head chef who personally sources rare and limited-edition Kagoshima shochu from around the prefecture. Unusually, they also operate a sake shop — Sakahoya Wasou (酒穂屋和奏) — making them one of the most serious shochu destinations in the city. Tenmonkan is Kagoshima's historic tram-served entertainment district, named after an Edo-period astronomical observatory (天文館) built by Shimazu Shigehide in 1779.

photos.caption: 大海綾紫 (Taikai Ayamurasaki) — shirokoji, crisp and citrusy
Monthly specials board — handwritten illustrations of rare bottles
華吟 (Hanagin) by Toyoshizagura — ginjo yeast shochu

entries.name: Lovely at Café Minamikaze
entries.name_ja: あつあつ焼きたてラブリー at カフェ南風
entries.our_notes: Stopped off on the way back from buying a blender in Kagoshima Chuo. Great value for a cake and drink set at 660 yen. Nice little find — sweet potato tart fresh from the oven, served with ice cream and a coffee.
entries.our_notes_ja: 鹿児島中央でブレンダーを買った帰り道に立ち寄りました。ケーキとドリンクのセットが660円でとてもお得。焼きたてのさつまいものタルトにアイスクリームとコーヒー。思いがけない素敵なお店でした。
entries.background: Festivalo is a local Kagoshima chain known for its freshly baked sweet potato sweets. Kagoshima sweet potato (薩摩芋) has been the prefecture's signature produce for over 400 years — introduced via Okinawa from Southeast Asia in the early 1600s and once used as famine insurance across Japan. The famous Satsuma variety remains one of Japan's most prized.

photos.caption: あつあつ焼きたてラブリー — sweet potato tart with ice cream, 660¥
